title: Rising mercury level troubles residents in Surat: Heatwave like conditions & humidity with 40°C temperature... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Rising mercury level troubles residents in Surat:Heatwave-like conditions & humidity with 40°C temperature; elderly and children urged to stay indoors
- Copy link
In the middle of April itself, the residents of Surat city have started facing scorching heat conditions. With the maximum temperature being recorded at 39.8 degrees Celsius in the last twenty-four hours, stepping outdoor is not at all advisable for elderly and children.
The hot winds can be witnessed from the early morning itself. According to the Meteorological Department, the situation may further deteriorate with rising mercury levels in the coming days.
Condition worsened by humidity and stuffiness
Being a coastal city, the humidity level in Surat always remains challenging along with the heat. In the last twenty-four hours, the minimum temperature in the city was 25.6 degrees, but with the humidity level (RH) recorded at a high of 80 percent, the city residents have been crying out in distress from the unbearable stuffiness.
With wind speed from the south-southwest (SSW) direction at only 4 km per hour, there has been no relief from the heat. Due to atmospheric stability and high humidity, the temperature is being felt at more than 42 degrees.
Demand for cold beverages increased
Due to the wrath of the scorching heat, between 12 noon to 4 PM, less traffic was observed on the city's main roads in Varachha, Adajan and Vesu areas. People are avoiding going out without work.
To escape the heat, people are resorting to cold beverages like sugarcane juice, buttermilk and lemon water. The Municipal Corporation has also intensified work to arrange drinking water facilities and install green nets at public places in view of the heatwave risk.
Temperature statistics in last twenty-four Hours
Maximum Temperature: 39.8C Minimum Temperature: 25.6C Humidity Level: 80 percent Wind Direction/Speed: SSW/04 kmph
Necessary to Take Precautions to Avoid Heat Stroke
Doctors at Civil and SMIMER Hospital have appealed to citizens to take special precautions. Cases of dehydration and heat stroke are likely to increase in the rising heat.
Doctors have advised to avoid going out in direct sunlight during afternoon hours, wear cotton clothes and keep drinking water continuously. It is advisable especially for elderly people and children not to go out during afternoon hours.
